% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid size
% ------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_) }.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Border helpers (0‑based indices)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row,_,_)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_,Col,_)}.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Adjacent moves (4‑connected)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. “Boundary” predicate for a colour
%    – a colour W is a boundary colour iff some input cell
%      on the grid border has colour W.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
boundary_exists(W) :- input(R,C,V), V = W, (R = Rmin, min_row(Rmin)).
boundary_exists(W) :- input(R,C,V), V = W, (R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax)).
boundary_exists(W) :- input(R,C,V), V = W, (C = Cmin, min_col(Cmin)).
boundary_exists(W) :- input(R,C,V), V = W, (C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax)).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Choose the unique wall colour.
%    Exactly one colour that appears on a border cell will be chosen.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { Wall : color(W), boundary_exists(W) } 1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Flood‑fill the “outside” region.
%    Cells that are on the border *and* are NOT the wall colour start the
%    flood fill; the flood propagates through cells that are also NOT the wall.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != Wall,
    (R = Rmin, min_row(Rmin)   ;  R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax)
    (C = Cmin, min_col(Cmin)   ;  C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax)).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V2), V2 != Wall.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Output cell definition – a tiled copy of the whole input.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :-
    input(BR,BC,_), input(DR,DC,_),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR,  C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Correct colour for every output cell
%    – wall cells stay the wall colour,
%    – outside non‑wall cells become 0,
%    – inside non‑wall cells become the wall colour.
% ------------------------------------------------------------
:- input(R,C,V), V = Wall, output(R,C,Col), Col != Wall.
:- outside(R,C), output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
:- not outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != Wall,
   output(R,C,Col), Col != Wall.
</asp>

output_cell2(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC) :-
    R = BR * N + DR,
    C = BC * N + DC,
    BR >= 0, BR < N,
    BC >= 0, BC < N,
    DR >= 0, DR < N,
    DC >= 0, DC < N,
    input(BR,BC,_),
    input(DR,DC,_).

% Exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell2(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC).

% Allowed colours
color(0..9).

% Enforce correct colour for every output cell

% (i) any colour other than 0 or Wall is forbidden
:- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != Wall => false.

% (ii) if the anchor cell is zero the whole block must be 0
:- output(R,C,Col), output_cell2(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC), input(BR,BC,0), Col != 0 => false.

% (iii) corner of any block (DR = 0, DC = 0) must be Wall
:- output(R,C,Col), output_cell2(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC), DR = 0, DC = 0, Col != Wall => false.

% (iv) first column of a block (BR = 0, DC = 0) must be Wall
:- output(R,C,Col), output_cell2(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC), BR = 0, DC = 0, Col != Wall => false.

% (v) first row of a block (BC = 0, DR = 0) must be Wall
:- output(R,C,Col), output_cell2(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC), BC = 0, DR = 0, Col != Wall => false.

% (vi) bottom‑right sub‑block of interior blocks must be Wall
:- output(R,C,Col), output_cell2(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC), BR > 0, BC > 0, DR >= BR, DC >= BC, Col != Wall => false.
</asp>

max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R,_,_) }.

% Dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_) }.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Border positions (for wall colour)
min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min{R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R,_,_) }.
min_col(Cmin) :- Cmin = #min{C : input(_,C,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Wall colour – the only non‑zero colour on the border
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (R = Rmin, min_row(Rmin)).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax)).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (C = Cmin, min_col(Cmin)).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax)).

1 { Wall : color(W) } 1 :- boundary_color(W).

% Position of a cell inside a block (i from 0 to N‑2)
block_pos(R,C) :-
    n_rows(N),
    I >= 0,
    I < N-1,
    R >= I * (N+1) + 1,
    R <= I * (N+1) + 1 + N,
    C >= I * (N+1) + 1,
    C <= I * (N+1) + 1 + N.

% Every output position must be defined
output_cell(R,C) :- block_pos(R,C).

% Choose a colour for each output cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% Enforce correct colour: inside a block → Wall, outside → 0
:- output(R,C,Col), output_cell(R,C), Col != Wall => false.
:- output(R,C,Col), not output_cell(R,C), Col != 0 => false.

% Exactly one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,C1), output(R,C,C2), C1 != C2 :- false.

% Ensure output size matches input size (implicitly by definition of output cells)

% dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_) }.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% output range
Rmax := N * N - 1.
Cmax := N * N - 1.

% block predicate
block_pos(R,C) :-
    n_rows(N),
    I >= 0,
    I < N-1,
    R >= I * (N+1) + 1,
    R <= I * (N+1) + 1 + N,
    C >= I * (N+1) + 1,
    C <= I * (N+1) + 1 + N.

% output cell definition (within bounds)
output_cell(R,C) :-
    R >= 0, R <= Rmax,
    C >= 0, C <= Cmax.

% enumeration: exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% correct colour constraints
:- output(R,C,Col), block_pos(R,C), Col != Wall => false.
:- output(R,C,Col), not block_pos(R,C), Col != 0 => false.

% exactly one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,C1), output(R,C,C2), C1 != C2 :- false.

% choose wall colour (unique non‑zero border colour)
min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min{R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R,_,_) }.
min_col(Cmin) :- Cmin = #min{C : input(_,C,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.

boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (R = Rmin, min_row(Rmin)).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax)).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (C = Cmin, min_col(Cmin)).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax)).

1 { Wall : color(W) } 1 :- boundary_color(W).

% Grid dimensions
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R,_,_) }.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% block predicate
block_pos(R,C) :-
    n_rows(NR),
    I >= 0,
    I < NR-1,
    R >= I * (NR+1),
    R <= I * (NR+1) + NR,
    C >= I * (NR+1),
    C <= I * (NR+1) + NR.

% output cells (within grid bounds)
output_cell(R,C) :-
    R >= 0,
    R < NR * NR,
    C >= 0,
    C < NC * NC.

% enumerate colours (exactly one per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% enforce correct colour
:- output(R,C,Col), block_pos(R,C), Col != Wall => false.
:- output(R,C,Col), not block_pos(R,C), Col != 0 => false.

% exactly one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,C1), output(R,C,C2), C1 != C2 => false.

% wall colour (unique non‑zero colour on the border)
min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min{R : input(R,_,_) }.
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R,_,_) }.
min_col(Cmin) :- Cmin = #min{C : input(_,C,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.

boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (R = Rmin, min_row(Rmin)).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax)).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (C = Cmin, min_col(Cmin)).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax)).

1 { Wall : color(W) } 1 :- boundary_color(W).

% Compute dimensions
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R,_,_) }.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% Determine Wall colour (unique non‑zero border colour)
min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min{R : input(R,_,_)}.
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
min_col(Cmin) :- Cmin = #min{C : input(_,C,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_,C,_) }.

boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (R = Rmin, min_row(Rmin)).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax)).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (C = Cmin, min_col(Cmin)).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, (C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax)).

1 { Wall : color(W) } 1 :- boundary_color(W).

% Compute offset = minimum row index of a wall cell
wall_row(R) :- input(R,C,V), V = Wall.
min_wall_row(Offset) :- wall_row(R), R = Rmin, R = #min{R : wall_row(R)}.

% Block 0 (i = 0)
block0(R,C) :-
    min_wall_row(Off),
    Off >= 0,
    Off < NR,
    R >= Off,
    R <= Off + NR,
    C >= Off,
    C <= Off + NR.

% Block i > 0 (i >= 1) if there is a wall at (i,0) or (0,i)
block1(I,R,C) :-
    min_wall_row(Off),
    Off >= 0,
    Off < NR,
    I >= 1,
    I < NR-1,
    (input(I,0,Wall) ; input(0,I,Wall)),
    R >= Off + I * (NR+1),
    R <= Off + I * (NR+1) + NR,
    C >= Off + I * (NR+1),
    C <= Off + I * (NR+1) + NR.

% A cell belongs to a block if it is covered by block0 or block1
block_pos(R,C) :- block0(R,C).
block_pos(R,C) :- block1(I,R,C).

% Enumerate output colours (exactly one per cell)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- block_pos(R,C).

% Enforce correct colour
:- output(R,C,Col), block_pos(R,C), Col != Wall => false.
:- output(R,C,Col), not block_pos(R,C), Col != 0 => false.

% Exactly one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,C1), output(R,C,C2), C1 != C2 :- false.

% Ensure output size bounds
R >= 0.
R < NR * NR.
C >= 0.
C < NC * NC.

% No further declarations.
